data.title: AMD Gets Catalyst Watch From Citi
data.description:
Citi has put Advanced Micro Devices (AMD, Financials) on a 30-day positive catalyst watch. This is because they expect demand to rise because of artificial intelligence and the growth of data centers.

The company said that demand for CPUs is going up since the market is moving toward inference and agentic AI workloads, which need more processing power. Citi says that hyperscaler capital expenditures would go up by 69% this year, and the entire addressable market will reach $731 billion by 2028.

It was also pointed out that Analog Devices will benefit from an estimated 10% to 15% rise in analog prices due to higher input costs. Even if there is a lot of uncertainty in the economy as a whole, the pricing environment suggests that major semiconductor areas will continue to do well.

Citi analysts also noted that global manufacturing indicators are holding up, especially in computing and communications equipment. This suggests that AI infrastructure investment is helping the industry stay active.

Smartphone shipments are likely to drop 17% this year because memory prices are going up. However, companies that work with Apple's high-end ecosystem, like Skyworks and Qualcomm, should be reasonably safe.

The next thing that will excite investors' interest is the future earnings reports. These will show whether AI-driven demand leads to long-term revenue growth and higher profit margins.

data.title: My Neighbor Alice to Hold Giveaway
data.description:
Alice announced the launch of Alice Prize Draw, a daily raffle in the Archipelago. Users can buy tickets for a chance to win the full prize, with larger bets increasing the prize pool and more tickets improving the odds of winning. The draw will take place every day at 12:00 UTC, once on Ethereum and once on BNB.

Refer to the official tweet by ALICE:

Alice
@MyNeighborAlice

We know our Neighbors know how to build big time and, from today onward, they can test their luck as well in the Archipelago 😎

Introducing the Alice Prize Draw, a daily raffle where our Neighbors can win big and get the full prize! The more the bets, the bigger the prize â†’ theâ€¦ pic.twitter.com/HEklSmtaHG

Apr 06, 2026

ALICE Info

My Neighbor Alice is a multiplayer builder-game that merges the world of gaming with blockchain. Players can construct virtual lands, interact with others, and receive rewards. The game, drawing inspiration from mainstream games like Animal Crossing, offers both a regular gameplay narrative and a system for players to acquire and trade NFTs.

Central to My Neighbor Alice’s ethos is its community-driven governance. The game features a DAO, named The Community Council, which empowers ALICE token holders to engage actively in the decision-making processes. This includes areas such as game progression, validation of NFTs, and orchestrating various competitions. Moreover, the revenue generated is channeled back to the community, ensuring a continuous cycle of development and engagement. Additionally, DeFi components are seamlessly woven into the game, offering players incentives and enriching the gameplay experience.

ALICE is the inherent utility token for the game and serves several crucial functions. Within the game, it acts as the primary currency, facilitating the purchase of assets and unique abilities. It also plays a pivotal role in governance, enabling token holders to have a say in the game“s direction through the DAO. Furthermore, staking opportunities are available, allowing users to generate a portion of the game”s revenue. Lastly, as a means of engagement and retention, players can earn ALICE tokens by completing various in-game quests.

data.title: Oil Prices Push Higher Ahead of Trump's Hormuz Deadline — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Dezember

Oil futures ticked higher Monday ahead of a Tuesday night deadline President Trump has set for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to tanker traffic or face a new wave of attacks on its power plants and bridges.

  • U.S. crude futures settled Monday 0.8% higher to end at $112.41 a barrel, the highest price since June 2022 and up 68% since the U.S. and Israel began bombing Iran at the end of February.
  • Prices at the pump continued to rise Monday. The national average price for regular unleaded gasoline hit roughly $4.12 a gallon, up from $2.98 before the fighting began in the Persian Gulf. Diesel prices rose to about $5.62, from $3.76 in late February.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson steps down, Mint reports
data.description:
Air India CEO Campbell Wilson has stepped down from his position, Indian newspaper Mint reported, citing two people with knowledge of the matter

The airline has been grappling with persistent losses and heightened regulatory scrutiny following a crash last year that killed 260 people.

Wilson is currently serving his notice period, the report said, adding the newspaper could not immediately ascertain if the Tata group has found his successor.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

data.title: Tech Giants Face Growing Legal Pressure on Content and AI
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META, Financials) and Alphabet (GOOG, Financials) are entering a legal climate that is less certain than it has been in the past 30 years.

Section 230 is a statute that protects platforms from being sued for information created by users. Companies like Meta and Google have used it for years. But that protection is being put to the test in new ways, and recent court rulings hint that the law may be changing.

The way these instances are being framed is changing. Lawsuits are starting to go after how platforms are built, like recommendation algorithms, notifications, and even AI-generated responses, instead of just the material that users upload.

That difference is important. If courts start to see these features as conduct caused by the company instead of neutral hosting, it could lead to additional legal problems.

Recent decisions against Meta and YouTube, as well as fresh instances using Google's AI capabilities, indicate how swiftly this change is happening. The financial fines so far have been small, but the main worry is that this sets a bad example.

This is less about what will happen right away and more about the risk over the long term for investors. As AI grows more important to these platforms, the law may pay greater attention to them as well.

data.title: Tesla South Korea Sales Jump in March
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA, Financials) posted a sharp rise in South Korean vehicle registrations in March, underscoring the impact of recent price cuts on its China-made electric vehicles.

The U.S. automaker registered 11,134 vehicles in South Korea during the month, up 330% from a year earlier, according to market researcher Carisyou. The gain marks a significant rebound in one of Asia's more competitive electric vehicle markets.

Tesla lowered the cost of certain of its Model Y and Model 3 cars made in China, which led to the rise. The cutbacks seem to have helped boost demand while putting more pressure on other electric vehicle manufactures in Korea, where prices are becoming more competitive.

The rise in sales in March is important because it shows that Tesla can still utilize price to gain market share, even while competition is growing and demand for electric vehicles is uneven around the world. Stronger registrations in South Korea may help make investors feel better by showing that lowering prices can lead to more sales.

Investors will probably keep an eye on whether Tesla can keep up that momentum over the next few months and whether any changes in pricing effect margins and demand trends in different areas.

data.title: Soleno Stock Soars 32% on $2.9 Billion Neurocrine Buyout. Why Its Hunger Drug Is a Different Weight-Loss Play. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni and Jack Denton

Neurocrine Biosciences struck a nearly $3 billion deal to acquire Soleno Therapeutics, giving it access to a drug targeting extreme hunger. But it isn't the kind of weight-loss medicine investors might expect.

San Diego-based Neurocrine announced Monday that it is buying Soleno Therapeutics for $53 a share, or roughly $2.9 billion. For Neurocrine, the transaction accelerates its portfolio diversification strategy and grows revenue.

The implied deal value represents a roughly 34% premium to Soleno's closing price of $39.49 on Thursday, and a 51% premium to Soleno's 30-day volume-weighted average price. The company had a market capitalization of $2 billion as of the end of Thursday's session.

Shares surged another 32% to $52.29 on Monday following the deal announcement. Neurocrine stock reversed earlier losses and advanced 0.8%. The benchmark S&P 500 was up 0.3%.

Cantor analyst Kristen Kluska noted Monday that the deal price falls below Soleno's 52-week high of $90.32, but is "substantially higher" than the levels the stock traded at before a positive randomized withdrawal period study readout in September 2023.

Soleno had just a $44 million market cap at the time it reported positive, statistically significant top-line results in a trial evaluating the efficacy of its diazoxide choline extended-release tablets.

The deal gives Neurocrine access to this very asset, which has since been commercialized as Vykat XR, the first drug treating insatiable hunger in patients living with Prader-Willi syndrome.

Vykat XR isn't a weight-loss drug in the vein of Eli Lilly's tirzepatide or Novo Nordisk's semaglutide, which are sold under respective brand names for chronic weight management or the treatment of type 2 diabetes.

Rather, Soleno's drug, which received U.S. regulatory approval in March 2025, targets patients as young as 4 years old living with a very specific genetic disorder that's linked to obesity.

Prader-Willi syndrome stems from the loss of function of specific genes on the paternal copy of chromosome 15. The disorder causes constant, relentless hunger through hypothalamic dysfunction, where the brain fails to register fullness, often leading to life-threatening obesity.

Kluska noted that investors "have heavily focused on thinking about new patient start forms and overall growth." She expects this trend to continue, "given the seriousness of this condition and the potential with Vykat XR."

Recent conversations with families successfully being treated with Vykat XR "gave us a glimpse of how complex PWS is and how parents are willing to consider the benefit/risk profile in light of this," the analyst added.

The implied deal value of $2.9 billion is above Cantor's estimate for U.S. peak sales of around $2 billion. The firm would value the stock at roughly $90 considering the U.S. market opportunity alone. This implies 35% market penetration before factoring in any discontinuation rates, versus the 10% the company is at today.

Vykat XR awaits regulatory approval in the European Union, with a decision expected later this year. Kluska noted that Soleno recently received its Day 180 questions from the European Medicines Agency, or the final set of queries that must be answered in order to secure a marketing authorization.

All things considered, "we do believe the valuation is low relative to the potential we believe Vykat XR has in the U.S. alone," Kluska wrote.

The deal has been approved by the boards of both companies and is expected to close within the next 90 days. Neurocrine will fund the transaction with cash on hand, and plans to "optimize its capital structure" by assuming a modest amount of pre-payable debt.

The deal helps Neurocrine's leaders in "delivering transformative medicines," CEO Kyle Gano said in a statement.

The biotechnology firm already has two drugs on the market: Ingreeza, a treatment for involuntary muscle movements associated with Huntington's disease, and Crenessity, which was approved in December 2024 for the treatment of classic congenital adrenal hyperplasia, a serious inherited disorder stemming an enzyme deficiency.

"We share the Soleno team's deep commitment to the Prader-Willi syndrome community and look forward to leveraging our experience and capabilities to expand Vykat XR's reach to benefit more patients," Gano said.

The transaction could lift the struggling stock. While shares have surged 38% over the past 12 months, narrowly outpacing a 30% gain for the S&P 500, they've shed 6.8% this year. The benchmark index, by comparison, has fallen 3.6%.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com and Jack Denton at jack.denton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Strategy Reports $14.46B Bitcoin Loss While Adding $330M More BTC
data.description:
Strategy is leaning further into bitcoin at a time when its balance sheet is still reflecting a sizable mark-to-market drawdown. The company disclosed it acquired $330M of bitcoin (BTC-USD) between April 1 and April 5, adding 4,871 coins at an average price of $67,718. This comes alongside a reported $14.46B unrealized loss on digital assets at the end of Q1 2026, partially offset by a $2.42B deferred tax benefit tied to those losses. Despite that accounting pressure, the stock moved higher in premarket trading, rising 3.9%, which could suggest investors are focusing more on the accumulation strategy than the near-term volatility in reported earnings.

The funding mechanics behind the latest purchases reinforce how Strategy continues to scale exposure through capital markets. The company sold approximately 2.28M shares of variable rate series A perpetual Stretch preferred stock for $227.3M of net proceeds across March 3031, followed by another 1.03M shares raising $102.6M between April 1 and April 6. It also issued 593,294 shares of class A common stock, generating an additional $72.0M. This combination of preferred and common issuance appears designed to keep capital flowing into bitcoin accumulation, though it could also introduce incremental dilution and financing complexity over time.

Following these transactions, Strategy now holds 766,970 bitcoins acquired at an average price of $75,644, keeping it among the largest corporate holders of the asset. With bitcoin recently trading around $69.4K, up roughly 0.6% over the last 24 hours, the company's aggregate position remains below its average cost basis, which could keep unrealized losses elevated if prices do not move higher. At the same time, the continued buying activity may signal management's longer-term positioning around bitcoin, even as near-term financial results remain sensitive to price fluctuations.

data.title: Micron Stock Jumps After Bullish Call-- KeyBanc Sees 60% Upside
data.description:
A KeyBanc analyst raised earnings projections for Micron on Monday and kept a buy rating on the memory chip company.

The stock gained about 3% trading following the update. Analyst John Vinh set a $600 price target, which suggests the shares could climb roughly 60% from where they recently traded.

Vinh now forecasts earnings of $64.37 per share for the company's 2026 fiscal year, up from an earlier estimate of $60.18. For fiscal 2027, the projection moved higher to $131.38 per share from $118.49. The analyst cited solid demand for processors and memory products as a primary factor behind the increases.

Micron makes two main types of memory chips. NAND handles long-term storage in devices like smartphones and solid-state drives. DRAM serves as temporary working memory in computers and data center servers.

A recent announcement from Google about a new compression algorithm added further pressure. The technology improves AI efficiency and cuts memory needs. Some analysts, however, believe better AI efficiency could eventually boost overall memory demand.

Vinh also noted that pricing for DRAM and NAND may rise 30% to 50% in the current quarter. New multi-year supply deals with major cloud providers include pricing floors and upfront payments, which could limit downside during industry downturns.

data.title: Artemis Astronauts Make History — WSJ
data.description:
By Micah Maidenberg

The Artemis astronauts are set to go the distance — the longest in human history, in fact.

A few minutes before 2 p.m. ET Monday, the astronauts on NASA's Artemis II lunar mission are expected to enter the record books when the Orion craft transporting them flies more than 248,655 miles from Earth.

At that point, the four crew members will have journeyed farther beyond the planet than anyone else, breaking the distance milestone set in 1970 with NASA's Apollo 13 mission.

Monday is poised to be one of the most consequential periods for the Artemis II mission, which has proceeded with only minor glitches so far.

Earlier in the day, Orion entered a point where the gravity of the moon was exerting a stronger pull on the spacecraft than the Earth, setting up the crew — Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen — for the kind of lunar flyby NASA hasn't attempted in decades.

Around 6:44 p.m. ET Monday, Orion will transit behind the moon, where the crew will lose communications with Earth for 40 minutes or so. Shortly after that, the ship will reach its maximum distance from the planet during the mission, 252,757 miles.

Joining the dark side

Besides closing in on a new distance record for human spaceflight, the Artemis crew has been gearing up to make the most of the time they spend swooping around the moon. That means meticulously photographing and observing the lunar surface.

"We're looking for the crew to take time during their flyby, let their eyes adjust to what they're seeing, and call out any of those subtle color nuances — especially on parts of the far side that never have been seen before by human eyes," Kelsey Young, the top lunar science official for the mission, said Saturday at a briefing.

Mission controllers sent the crew a list of 30 targets to track on the lunar surface. Among them is a 3.8 billion-year-old crater called Orientale Basin, and a different crater to its northwest.

Young said the crew trained extensively for the flyby, including classroom drills on "description practice" and a field trip to Iceland to work on describing geologic features.

Toilet turbulence

A few issues cropped up on Orion over the past couple of days, including an unusual smell in the spacecraft. It wasn't clear what was causing the odor, NASA officials said, which may have emerged from a mechanical system or materials like tapes onboard.

Mission controllers also spent time working to figure out why a wastewater vent line on the ship's toilet wasn't functioning as expected. The part probably was getting blocked by ice build up, officials said, leading the crew to use other devices to urinate.

"It's a little bit of camping in space already, but then it makes the camping a little bit tougher when you don't have the full capability of the toilet," said John Honeycutt, chairman of the Artemis II mission management team, at a Saturday briefing.

NASA officials said Sunday they were still working on the vent line.

Write to Micah Maidenberg at micah.maidenberg@wsj.com

GLOBAL - Equities rose modestly while oil prices edged lower in a choppy session on Monday as investors awaited signs of a possible resolution to the Middle East war, as the U.S. and Iran weighed the framework of a plan to end their conflict.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI rose 1.94 points, or 0.20%, to 996.14.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - Wall Street's main indexes kicked off the week on a higher note on Monday after marking their biggest weekly jump in four months in the previous session, while investors assessed prospects of an end to the Middle East conflict.

At 11:50 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 83.20 points, or 0.18%, to 46,587.48, the S&P 500 gained 16.71 points, or 0.25%, to 6,599.40 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 88.32 points, or 0.40%, to 21,967.50.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

The pan-European STOXX 600 index dipped 0.2% to 596.63 points on Thursday, after falling as much as 1.6% earlier in the session.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

The Nikkei rose 0.55% to 53,413.68.

Reuters will resume coverage of the Australia and New Zealand stock markets on Tuesday, April 7.

The benchmark KOSPI closed up 73.03 points, or 1.36%, at 5,450.33, after rising more than 2% earlier in the session.

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

The dollar index , which measures the U.S. currency against six others, edged down to 99.809. With many Asian and European markets closed on Monday, liquidity is likely to remain thin.

In midmorning trading, the benchmark 10-year yield, which falls when Treasury prices rise, was down slightly at 4.341% . Last week, the yield posted its largest weekly drop since February 23.

data.title: New Medicare Payment Rate Expected. What It Means for Healthcare Stocks. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Catherine Dunn

The federal government is expected on Monday to say how much it will pay insurers in the Medicare program next year, a highly anticipated financial data point for some of the country's largest health insurance companies.

A small proposed rate increase of 0.09%, announced in late January, caught the industry by surprise and shares of UnitedHealth Group and Humana fell on the news. Now Wall Street is watching for any improvement in the final rate notice.

It wouldn't be unusual for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services to release a higher final rate compared with the initial proposal. Still, "we see a more tepid rate" in the range of a 1% to 1.5% bump, wrote TD Cowen's Ryan Langston in a Monday morning note.

Medicare Advantage is the privately-run option for seniors to access Medicare health benefits — and it's a big source of business for insurers.

About 35 million beneficiaries are in Medicare Advantage plans this year, according to the health research group KFF. Enrollment in the privately-run plans has steadily grown to surpass enrollment in traditional Medicare plans operated by the government.

Insurers and their trade groups have pushed for a higher rate increase over the last couple of months, saying the proposal doesn't reflect medical cost trends.

"A near-zero payment update when medical costs are rising 7% to 9% a year does not hold the program steady," the Better Medicare Alliance said last month. "It functions as a cut."

At the same time, bipartisan calls to curb Medicare Advantage spending have added pressure to the rate dialogue. Republicans and Democrats have both raised concerns about practices that allow insurers to collect higher payments for beneficiaries with more recorded medical diagnoses.

Under the Biden administration, the Medicare agency began to tighten payments tied to coding, also known as risk scores or risk adjustment. The agency's January rate proposal, now under the Trump administration, signaled a willingness to continue such scrutiny.

Write to Catherine Dunn at catherine.dunn@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AMC, Cinemark Stocks Jump After Record Easter Weekend. Thank Super Mario. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

AMC Entertainment shares were rising early Monday after the movie-theater company said it posted its biggest-ever revenue for a five-day Easter weekend. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is driving attendance.

AMC shares were up 9.8% in early trading. The company said more than six million customers attended its locations in the U.S. or internationally from April 1 through April 5.

"THE SUPER MARIO GALAXY MOVIE brought families, younger audiences, and longtime fans to our theatres in impressive numbers, in the highest grossing Easter holiday weekend ever for AMC and ODEON," AMC CEO Adam Aron said in a statement.

Universal Pictures — owned by Comcast — and Nintendo notched the biggest box office weekend of the year with The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, with an estimated $372.5 million in global ticket sales through Sunday.

Comcast stock was up 0.5% in early trading, while Nintendo rose 1.3% in Tokyo trading.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Wall St clings to gains as investors assess US-Iran negotiations, threats
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes up: Dow 0.03%, S&P 500 0.09%, Nasdaq 0.14%
  • March job gains surpass expectations, unemployment rate dips
  • ISM non-manufacturing PMI decelerates, priced paid hit 3-1/2-year high
  • Soleno Therapeutics surges on Neurocrine's $2.9 bln buyout deal

By Stephen Culp and Purvi Agarwal

Wall Street edged higher on Monday as investors looked for signs of progress toward a U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal amid President Donald Trump's progressively heated threats, should Iran fail to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Iran has rejected the U.S. proposal for an immediate ceasefire, insisting instead on a permanent end to the war, according to the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA). The rejection followed Trump's increasingly bellicose ultimatums, vowing to rain "hell" on Iran if the crucial Strait of Hormuz bottleneck remains closed to oil tanker traffic.

Investors drew some reassurance from a report that indicated the U.S., Iran and a group of regional mediators continued to discuss terms of a potential ceasefire.

All three major U.S. indexes advanced slightly, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq on track for the fourth consecutive day of gains.

"On a day like today investors don’t do much of anything," said Thomas Martin, senior portfolio manager at GLOBALT in Atlanta. "We don't know where the truth lies with any of the stories really, whether it's on the U.S. side, the Iran side, or the Pakistan side."

Martin said, "Trump really has to get the Strait of Hormuz open ... he's made a big deal about this one," and added that investors "are all waiting on pins and needles."

The U.S.-Israeli war on Iran has roiled markets for a little over a month. Spiking crude prices stoked inflation fears, and stocks have tumbled. Even though the S&P was on track for its fourth consecutive session of gains, the bellwether index remains down 4% since the conflict began.

Economic data on Monday showed the U.S. services sector expanded at a slower-than-expected pace in March, even as employment contracted in the sector and prices paid, an inflation predictor, surged to its highest level since October 2022.

The much-anticipated March jobs report, released on the Good Friday market holiday, showed the U.S. economy added 178,000 jobs last month, nearly triple the 60,000 consensus, an upside surprise dampened by a revision of February's job losses, to 133,000 from 92,000.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 15.40 points, or 0.03%, to 46,519.35, the S&P 500 gained 5.96 points, or 0.09%, to 6,588.65 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 31.57 points, or 0.14%, to 21,910.75.

Of the 11 major sectors in the S&P 500, communication services enjoyed the largest percentage gains, while materials were the biggest laggards.

Travel/leisure stocks (.SPCOMHOTL) and aerospace & defense (.SPCOMAED) were clear outperformers.

Trading volumes on Monday were expected to be thin as many markets in Europe and Asia are closed for public holidays.

Rising bitcoin prices helped U.S.-listed shares of cryptocurrency-linked firms Coinbase and Strategy advance 1.8% and 3.9%, respectively.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.47-to-1 ratio on the NYSE. There were 68 new highs and 41 new lows on the NYSE.

On the Nasdaq, 2,787 stocks rose and 1,843 fell as advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.51-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 7 new 52-week highs and one new low while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 50 new highs and 56 new lows.

data.title: QDRO Acquisition completes $200M IPO, places $200M in trust and sells 6M private warrants
data.description:
QDRO Acquisition consummated a $200.0 million IPO of 20,000,000 units and placed $200.0 million in a trust account.

Key Highlights:

  • Sold 20,000,000 units at $10.00 each in IPO; each unit includes one Class A share and one-half warrant.
  • Placed $200,000,000 of IPO proceeds in a U.S.-based trust account to be used for a future business combination.
  • Completed private placement of 6,000,000 warrants at $1.00 each, raising $6,000,000 from Sponsor and Cantor Fitzgerald.
  • Transaction costs totaled $12,902,142, including $8,000,000 deferred underwriting fees held pending a business combination.
  • Audited balance sheet as of March 30, 2026 shows $201,293,513 total assets and a $7,075,270 shareholders’ deficit.

Original SEC Filing: QDRO Acquisition Corp. [ QADRU ] - 8-K - Apr. 06, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Injective to Hold April Community BuyBack on April 8
data.description:
Injective announced that its April Community BuyBack will take place on April 8. More than 51,000 INJ will be allocated for the event. The project said whitelisted participants will earn yield, while the buyback will remove INJ from circulation permanently.

Refer to the official tweet by INJ:

Injective 🥷
@injective

The April $INJ Community BuyBack is coming

51,000+ $INJ up for grabs. April 8, 2026.

Whitelisted participants earn yield while $INJ gets 🔥 from circulation forever pic.twitter.com/rKi7v9dxqE

Apr 06, 2026

INJ Info

Injective Protocol is a decentralized exchange (DEX) protocol built on the Cosmos SDK, offering advanced cross-chain trading capabilities.

Key features of Injective Protocol include:

1. Decentralization: On the Injective platform, users have full control over their funds, with no third-party intervention.

2. Cross-chain trading: Injective supports trading between different blockchains, providing users with a wide range of tradable assets.

3. High performance: Injective utilizes the Tendermint consensus system, ensuring high throughput and low transaction latency.

4. Gas-free transactions: Users can trade without the need to pay gas fees.

INJ is the native token of Injective Protocol. The token is used for protocol governance, staking, and participation in voting processes that address key protocol upgrades and management decisions.

data.title: Newport Gold Enters Final Stage of NFI Empire Share Exchange Merger
data.description:
Newport Gold (NWPG) is moving into the final phase of its planned merger with the group of Pennsylvania-based entities operating as NFI Empire, formalizing the process through a share exchange agreement that outlines how the two sides will combine. The structure is positioned as a more streamlined alternative to traditional reverse mergers, allowing the company to pursue expansion initiatives while potentially avoiding the dilution and structural complications that often come with those transactions.

Following what management described as a comprehensive review of strategic alternatives, CEO Anthony McCabe said the NFI transaction stood out as the strongest value proposition for shareholders. He pointed to NFI's existing operating base, noting it is already generating eight-figure revenue with positive profitability, which could provide Newport Gold with a more established and scalable foundation as the combined entity moves forward.

McCabe also highlighted NFI's leadership team and its defined roadmap for accelerated growth, suggesting these elements could support longer-term expansion and improved shareholder outcomes if execution remains on track. With the agreement now formalized, Newport Gold appears to be transitioning from evaluation to execution, a step that could reshape its operating profile as the merger progresses toward completion.

data.title: Jamie Dimon angles to become too big to lose
data.description:
By Stephen Gandel

The CEO of $800 bln JPMorgan already runs a bank that is too important to fail. After largely winning a massive regulatory battle, he insists remaining rules are “un-American.” Unfettered scale has benefits, but his vision is one of unassailable advantage for Wall Street titans.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Stephen Gandel on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon released his annual letter to shareholders on April 6.

data.title: Pentagon Budget Requests 85 F-35 Jets, Lifts Defense Spending 42%
data.description:
Lockheed Martin could be entering a renewed demand upswing as the Pentagon's proposed fiscal 2027 budget blueprint calls for 85 F-35 fighter jets, a sharp increase from the 47 requested last year. The Air Force remains the program's core customer, with plans for 38 F-35A jets, alongside 10 F-35B variants for the Marine Corps and 37 F-35C aircraft carrier models, according to the Office of Management and Budget. The scale of the increase suggests the F-35 program may continue to sit at the center of US defense priorities, with these figures likely to draw close scrutiny as lawmakers evaluate the broader budget framework.

The request sits within a much larger defense push from President Donald Trump, who is asking Congress to approve $1.5 trillion in defense spending, combining a $1.15 trillion base budget with an additional $350 billion expected through a separate reconciliation bill. Within that structure, 32 F-35 jets are expected to be funded through the base budget, with the remaining aircraft covered via reconciliation. The Pentagon would account for roughly 95% of the broader national security request, which the Office of Management and Budget says represents a 42% increase over enacted levels this fiscal year, while also lifting defense spending to about 4.5% of gross domestic product from around 3.2% last year.

The timing could further reinforce the program's strategic positioning. The US-Israeli war involving Iran is now in its second month, with F-35C jets deployed from the USS Abraham Lincoln already flying missions tied to operations over Iran. Beyond aircraft procurement, the budget outlines rising allocations across key categories, including about $260 billion for procurement and roughly $220 billion for research, development, testing and evaluation, compared with lower levels in the current fiscal request. A key focus is the Golden Dome missile defense initiative, which is set to receive $17.5 billion in research funding, largely through reconciliation, aimed at advancing space-based sensors, interceptors, and next-generation layered defense capabilities.
